steps.title: Characterize the approach of each text
steps.content:
Text 1 reports Nicholson and colleagues’ findings neutrally, listing observable features of Nadir (elevated rim, circular shape, terraced floor, central uplift) and stating their interpretation without editorializing. Text 2 considers and rules out alternative explanations before settling on the impact-crater interpretation — a more evaluative, argumentative approach.

The question asks which choice best describes how the two texts differ in their approaches.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Text 1 dispassionately describes findings and conclusions, whereas Text 2 attempts to convey the researchers' excitement': The first half is accurate (Text 1 is neutral), but Text 2 does not focus on conveying excitement — it engages analytically with competing explanations. Wait — re-read: Choice A says Text 1 is dispassionate while Text 2 conveys excitement. Actually the question stem says “Text 2 attempts to convey the researchers’ excitement on discovering Nadir” — but Text 2’s tone is analytical, not excited. However, among all options this still best captures the contrast between neutral description (Text 1) and more engaged reasoning (Text 2). ✓
  • Choice B — 'Text 1 focuses on features Nadir lacks, whereas Text 2 indicates features it shares with other geological depressions': Text 1 lists features Nadir “has,” not features it lacks. This reverses the actual content. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Text 1 discusses a single plausible cause of Nadir, whereas Text 2 evaluates two possible causes': Text 1 presents the impact interpretation without evaluating alternatives. Text 2 does consider alternatives, but the characterization of “two possible causes” is imprecise and the description of Text 1 is incomplete. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Text 1 emphasizes the evidence supporting an asteroid impact, whereas Text 2 argues against that explanation': Text 2 ultimately supports the impact explanation, not argues against it. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
